checks: Dashboard: stat cards, cluster cards, anomalies, filters visible
Cluster detail (test-cluster-minikube): nodes table, events, Last seen field
Cluster detail (prod-cluster-eks): unreachable, no Last seen shown
Clusters list page: both clusters with Last Seen column
Feature Flags page: 3 flags with targeting rules and activity logs
Audit Log: 259 entries with timestamps, users, actions
Settings: cluster connection, platform info, AI BYOK, API tokens, registered clusters
Dark mode toggle: functional, UI renders correctly in dark theme

findings.description: lastConnectedAt visible on cluster detail AND list pages. 'Last seen: 1m ago' shown correctly.
findings.evidence: test-cluster-minikube detail shows 'Last seen: 1m ago'; Clusters list table shows 'Last Seen: 1m ago' column for both clusters

findings.description: health-sync runs without contextName errors for minikube cluster (schema fix confirmed). BUT status is inconsistent across views: Dashboard card shows 'Degraded', Clusters list shows 'Unknown', Cluster detail shows 'Healthy'. DB status is not being updated from live data.
findings.evidence: logs show health-sync only logs for prod-cluster-eks (skipped - missing AWS creds). Minikube runs silently. Status discrepancy: Dashboard=Degraded, List=Unknown, Detail=Healthy

findings.description: Status inconsistency: same cluster (test-cluster-minikube) shows different health status across three views: 'Degraded' (dashboard cards), 'Unknown' (clusters list table), 'Healthy' (cluster detail header). Three different data sources/fields not in sync.
findings.evidence: Dashboard screenshot shows Degraded; Clusters table shows Unknown; Detail page shows Healthy

findings.description: Dashboard shows '0 LIVE · 2 REGISTERED' but test-cluster-minikube is live and healthy. The live counter is not being updated.
findings.evidence: Dashboard header text + detail page showing Healthy status with nodes

findings.description: Console error: SSE presence.subscribe endpoint returns text/plain instead of text/event-stream MIME type. ERR_INCOMPLETE_CHUNKED_ENCODING on /trpc/presence.subscribe
prod-cluster-eks health-sync skipped due to missing AWS credentials (accessKeyId, secretAccessKey undefined). Expected for EKS cluster without configured creds.
findings.evidence: [health-sync] cluster skipped — invalid connectionConfig with accessKeyId/secretAccessKey errors
